title: Still Looking for Work?
description:
Due to a corporate restructuring, Foxwell has some new vacancies that need to be filled immediately. Foxwell knows you’ve previously expressed interest in this line of work, and we’re wondering if you would want to take another crack at a contract.

One of our clients needs support at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> where their security forces are struggling to handle an outlaw incursion. Head there and help the local forces get rid of the problem before it’s too late. 

Successfully complete this contract, and Foxwell would be happy to send more work your way.


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Help Protect Site
description:
Got word that outlaws are attempting to overrun <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. There’s local security forces there, but they’re struggling to stop that attack.

Need a contractor to hustle there and help take care of the aggressors. Bring plenty of gear, and be careful when engaging targets. Don’t want to hear about any security team members getting caught in your crossfire.    


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Carefully Clear Site
description:
We’ve been contracted to take care of a delicate situation. A gang has settled into <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> that we need to get cleared out. Only issue is that there’s still some civilians there too. 

Looking for someone with the skills and good trigger sense to get rid of those outlaws without harming any innocent people. If that’s you, grab your gear and get over there. 


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Sweep and Clear Location
description:
Looking for someone willing to take out the trash at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. Guess a gang has settled in without the permission of the owners. So they’ve hired us to get them out whatever way we see fit. 

I’d bring plenty of ammo, if I were you. Doubt they’ll want to leave willingly. 


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Track Down ~mission(TargetName)
description:
Looking to execute a bounty on <EM4>~mission(TargetName)</EM4>. We’ve learned that they’re currently hiding out with some confederates at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. 

I’d be prepared to deal with the lowlifes hanging around <EM4>~mission(TargetName|Last)</EM4>, but our only real concern is that you land the target. 
    

Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Precision Strike on ~mission(TargetName)
description:
Got a line on a bounty named <EM4>~mission(TargetName)</EM4>. Someone spotted them along with known criminal accomplices at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. Need you to get there before they get going. 

<EM4>~mission(TargetName|Last)</EM4> has a reputation for only showing their face in places with civs. Really need you to take down this bounty without there being any civilian collateral damage. Foxwell can’t afford to be caught in the shitstorm that would erupt over the loss of innocent life. 


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Knock Out New Drug Op
description:
Some folks will do anything for a few creds. There’s a group of upstart outlaws over at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> that have decided to start manufacturing drugs. Turns out they’re not too good at it, or maybe a little too good. The junk they’re pumping out is stronger than the normal stuff and a real danger to anyone who uses it. 

We’ve been hired to knock out their operation and destroy whatever drugs stashes are on site. Armed guards are protecting the place, but they don’t seem too skilled. Our surveillance teams observed one of them almost shoot himself in the foot. Can’t say for certain, but odds are they won’t give a pro like you too many problems.  


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Destroy Illegal Drugs
description:
We’ve been contracted to take care of a problem at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. There’s been a recent surge of activity in the area tied to the drug trade. It appears to be a well-run operation and there’s a growing concern that it’ll expand wider if not stopped. Any chance you’re available to make sure that doesn’t happen?   

Job is pretty straightforward. Head to the site and destroy their stash of drugs. That way they won’t have the creds to expand their operations any further. I wouldn’t expect them to make this easy on you though. Armed guards are regularly seen around the facility, so be sure you’ve got enough gear to deal with them too. 


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Destroy Dangerous Drugs
description:
Reports of a spike in drug overdoses around Stanton has security services worried. Seems like some two-bit crew is juicing their profits by cutting their stuff with toxic chemicals. Shutting down this crew and stopping the distribution of their drugs has become a top priority.

A Foxwell informant has revealed that part of this crew has currently set up shop at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. Hiring a contractor to hustle over there and destroy whatever drugs they’ve got stashed before they’re spread across the system. Better be careful though, word is this crew is armed to the teeth and determined to profit off that poison no matter what.


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Exciting New Opportunities with Foxwell Enforcement
description:
Are you an experienced contractor looking to increase your income with some security work? Or maybe you’re new to the field and are looking for some hands-on experience? 

Foxwell Enforcement are looking for enthusiastic new contractors to help with local law enforcement. To see if you’re the right fit for us, we’d like for you to head over to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)<EM4> and support the security forces on site. Local gang members have been giving that spot problems. Help the local forces take care of these outlaws to qualify for more contract opportunities with Foxwell.

Contractors are employed on a strictly freelance basis. Foxwell expects contractors to supply their own weapons and armor and does not accept any responsibility for any injuries, damages, or deaths accumulated during the contract.

Good luck,

Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Red Level Contract: Clear PAFs, Recover Cargo, and Deliver to OLP
description:
Something weird is going on at the old Hathor mining facilities. Both the platform alignment facilities and orbital laser platforms have been abandoned and gathering dust for decades, but outlaws have started using them as a base of operations.

We’ve been hired by a concerned party who’s recently lost a lot of important cargo to one such gang. Currently, their stuff is stashed at <EM4>~mission(Location1|Address)</EM4> and <EM4>~mission(Location2|Address)</EM4>, but there’s a growing fear that the gang may be offloading their goods soon. That’s why we’ve been hired to hit both of those Hathor facilities, retrieve the cargo from the freight elevators, and bring it to the freight elevator at <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>. 

Here’s the specifics on what to grab from each location. Doesn’t matter in what order you do this, as long as it all gets collected and delivered. Just be sure to bring a ship that can handle <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize) cargo containers</EM4>.  It also wouldn't hurt to bring a <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> with you.   

~mission(MultiToSingleToken)

Foxwell hasn’t been able to scout these locations, but if I were you, I’d expect to face some serious armed resistance. Best bring a bunch of gear and some friends.


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Red Level Contract: Recoup Stolen Cargo Haul
description:
A crew recently raided a distribution center and made off with <EM4>~mission(CargoGradeToken) load of cargo</EM4>. Foxwell has been hired to get all those goods back. We’re getting reports of these outlaws using old Hathor facilities as stash houses and we believe the stolen items are currently at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>.

If interested, you’ll need a ship that can handle <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize) cargo containers</EM4>. Doubt you’ll be able to slip in and out of that PAF without being noticed either, so best be prepared to clear the location. Once safe, grab the goods from the freight elevator and deliver them to the <EM4>freight elevator at ~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>. 

It also wouldn't hurt to bring a <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> with you.   


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement
'Security, Your Way'

Foxwell Enforcement is a bonded security specialist licensed to operate throughout the UEE. We are always looking for new operators to join our family. If you or anyone you know is interested, please reach out to one of our contract representatives.

title: Red Level Contract: Retrieve Cargo Haul
description:
Looking for a contractor with the right skills and ship for an important mission. Outlaws ambushed a Hull C carrying a <EM4>~mission(CargoGradeToken)</EM4> load of cargo and took it to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. We’ve been hired to get it all back.  

The cargo is being stored somewhere at a defunct Hathor facility. The place is big and crawling with outlaws. Need someone to retrieve the goods from the freight elevator there and deliver it all back to the <EM4>freight elevator at ~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>. Don’t think this will be a walk in the park. Bring lots of ammo and a ship that can handle <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize) cargo containers</EM4>. 

It also wouldn't hurt to bring a <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> with you.   


Marcus Clenn
Foxwell Enforcement

description:

APPROVAL CODE = ~mission(ApprovalCode)

FOR IMMEDIATE PROCESSING

Eckhart Security is continuing to evaluate potential new contractors. According to our records, you’ve previously attempted a trial contract. We understand how demanding and unpredictable this line of work can be, and would like to give you another chance at completing a qualifying contract. 

If interested, report to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> ready to defend it from an incoming assault by outlaws. Successfully complete this contract to receive payment and more work opportunities from Eckhart Security.

This contract is non-negotiable.

title: High-Risk Bounty: ~mission(TargetName)
description:
ECKHART SECURITY, LLC.
CONTRACTS & DISPATCH

CONTRACT TYPE: Bounty
APPROVAL CODE = ~mission(ApprovalCode)

FOR IMMEDIATE PROCESSING

We’ve received actionable intelligence on a dangerous bounty, who’s turned out to be extremely elusive. <EM4>~mission(TargetName)</EM4> was spotted with a dedicated contingent of known associates at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. We’re looking for a contractor ready and willing to execute this bounty before they disappear again.

Based on previous attempts to apprehend this criminal, there’s a good chance that <EM4>~mission(TargetName|Last)</EM4> will try to avoid a direct confrontation, and instead send their allies to confront you. We believe that neutralizing these protective forces, and shredding the safety net they provide, is the best way to draw out <EM4>~mission(TargetName|Last)</EM4> to face you directly. 

Expect to confront a significant and skilled force. We advise that you plan accordingly. Payment will be issued upon successful completion of contract.

This contract is non-negotiable.

This message may contain confidential, proprietary, privileged and/or private information. The information is intended to be for the use of the individual or entity designated above.

title: Deal with ~mission(TargetName) and Support Forces
description:
ECKHART SECURITY, LLC.
CONTRACTS & DISPATCH

CONTRACT TYPE: Bounty
APPROVAL CODE = ~mission(ApprovalCode)

FOR IMMEDIATE PROCESSING

Got a big league bounty contract that’s primed to be executed. <EM4>~mission(TargetName)</EM4> and their crew were identified executing a raid on <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. If their MO holds true, <EM4>~mission(TargetName|Last)</EM4> has surfaced to land a big score and then will disappear into the ether. Let's not allow that to happen again. 

Word is the hostile forces have spread themselves across the location. That’ll make hunting down the precise location of <EM4>~mission(TargetName|Last)</EM4> difficult. In addition, expect the target to act defensively when under attack. In previous engagements, they remained hidden and directed their forces from afar. It is our recommendation that you focus on thinning out their allies first and force <EM4>~mission(TargetName|Last)</EM4> to come to their own defense. Would also advise you to bring plenty of ammo and even some support to help keep you from being overwhelmed.    

Payment will be issued upon successful completion of contract.

This contract is non-negotiable.

title: Inmate Maintenance Opportunity
description:
Hello Guests,

We have recently been alerted that one of our Oxygen Dispensers has suffered a technical error. As you know these kiosks are vital to ensure that you have plenty of oxygen handy while participating in our Work Release Program.

We are looking for any available guest to reset the kiosk. That should reconnect the kiosk to the network and resolve the issue. The first guest who's able to complete this task will not only be compensated with merits, but also with the satisfaction that they're making the workplace safer for other guests.

Thank you.

title: Eliminate Annoyance
description:
Headhunters flying in and out of <EM4>~mission(DefendLocationWrapperLocation|Address)</EM4> are being harassed by some aggressive upstarts. Need them dealt with before they become a real pain in the ass.   

Get there and I’ll update you on what these pests are doing so you can hunt ‘em down. 

Clear ‘em all out I’ll throw <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) Council Scrip</EM4> to whoever accepted this gig as a bonus.

- Stows

title: Protect Our Site
description:
Some idiots with a deathwish are attacking our ships around <EM4>~mission(DefendLocationWrapperLocation|Address)</EM4>. Can’t let ‘em do that without paying the price. Want to do the honors?

If so, head out and get after ‘em. The crew there is tracking the attacker’s movements and I’ll pass along situational updates as they come in. 

Also, I’ll sweeten the pot by giving the main contractor a bonus of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) Council Scrip</EM4> just to show my appreciation. 

Stows out.

title: Secure Our Airspace
description:
Our airspace around <EM4>~mission(DefendLocationWrapperLocation|Address)</EM4> has been compromised. Enemy ships are threatening the base and any of our ships in the area. Gotta put a stop to this immediately, so get your shit in order and get over there. Just get going and I’ll update you with any relevant info on where you’re needed. 
 
If you need any extra motivation, I’m adding a bonus <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) Council Scrip</EM4> for the main contractor.

- Stows

title: Help Headhunters at ~mission(DefendLocationWrapperLocation)
description:
Got word that Headhunters ships heading to <EM4>~mission(DefendLocationWrapperLocation|Address)</EM4> are coming under attack. Scans have revealed enemy forces in the wider region intent on targeting our ops. 

There’s enough of them that it’s gonna take more than one person to clear away all that trash. If you can grab your gear and a few friends, I could really use you out there.  

The specific attack areas and targets are evolving. Don’t worry about the details now. Just get there and I’ll let you know where you’re needed.  

Handle this right and I’ll give the main contractor a bonus <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) Council Scrip</EM4>.

title: Stop Attack at ~mission(DefendLocationWrapperLocation)
description:
Looking for someone who can not only dogfight but recruit others to the Headhunters cause. Our operations center at <EM4>~mission(DefendLocationWrapperLocation|Address)</EM4>, along with any ships going to and from it, are being targeted by hostile forces. We need support there pronto. 

Not gonna lie, but there’s lots of heat around there. That’s why you’ll need to be fully supplied and find some trustworthy friends to fly at your wing. When you get your crew together, head out there and I’ll update you if there’s any flare ups in the area that need your attention.

This op needs to go off without a hitch, so I’m offering the main contractor a bonus <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) Council Scrip</EM4> for a job well done. 

Stows out.

title: Restore Order at ~mission(DefendLocationWrapperLocation)
description:
Our crew at <EM4>~mission(DefendLocationWrapperLocation|Address)</EM4> needs help. Enemy ships are swarming the area and attacking anything and everything they can find. That’d be bad enough as is, but this site has some absolutely crucial gear and personnel that we can’t have destroyed or compromised. 

Considering the size and scale of the attack, you’re gonna need a fully supplied ship and some reliable wingmen. Gather your crew, fly out to the site, and I’ll send you specifics on where you’re needed most.

To prove just how serious we are about getting this done right, the main contractor will earn a bonus <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) Council Scrip</EM4> for making sure absolutely nothing goes wrong.

description:

title: Patrol Dangerous Sector
description:
MISSION SPECS

Area of Operation: Nyx System
Hostile Forces: Outlaws
Equipment Specs: Combat-rated Ships
Briefing: InterSec has received intelligence that outlaws are planning to launch an attack on a target within the UEE. In an effort to prevent this from happening, we need a contractor to patrol a sector we believe these outlaws are using to prepare for their attack.

Outlaw activity in this sector is higher than normal, so InterSec recommends that you <EM4>bring backup</EM4>. When ready, dispatch to the <EM4>location marker</EM4> on your HUD and scan the waypoints along the route. Once you've confirmed that the entire sector is clear, the main contractor will receive a bonus of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) MG Scrip.</EM4> 

Respectfully,

Deacon Tobin
Operations Manager
InterSec Defense Solutions

This communication consists of a non-binding offer that upon acceptance may be immediately terminated by either party without any given notice. All operations are conducted at the contractor’s own personal risk and must adhere to system law where applicable.

title: Patrol to Prevent Possible Attack
description:
MISSION SPECS

Area of Operation: Nyx System
Hostile Forces: Outlaws
Equipment Specs: Combat-rated Ships
Briefing: InterSec has been tracking increased outlaw activity in certain sectors of the system. This activity in conjunction with other intelligence leads us to believe that outlaws are planning an attack on elements traveling from the UEE. We're aiming to patrol these sectors and disrupt any plans before they can be put into action.

Currently, we need someone who can help patrol a highly dangerous sector. Once you've accepted the mission, you can proceed to the <EM4>location marker</EM4> on your HUD. From there, we'll send you along a specific route with additional waypoints to scan. Complete the patrol and we'll send the main contractor a bonus of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) MG Scrip.</EM4>

Due to the high probability of attack and general threat level of local outlaws, we recommend <EM4>recruiting trusted wingmen</EM4> to provide combat support.

Respectfully,

Deacon Tobin
Operations Manager
InterSec Defense Solutions

This communication consists of a non-binding offer that upon acceptance may be immediately terminated by either party without any given notice. All operations are conducted at the contractor’s own personal risk and must adhere to system law where applicable.

title: Patrol Vanduul Hunting Ground
description:
MISSION SPECS

Area of Operation: Nyx System
Hostile Forces: Vanduul
Equipment Specs: Combat-rated Ships
Briefing: There's a growing concern that Vanduul hunting parties are pushing further into Nyx. The first line of defense against this encroachment is increased patrols. InterSec has identified a sector that has seen an uptick in Vanduul activity and are looking for someone to patrol the region and provide an update on it.

You'll begin your patrol at a <EM4>location marker</EM4> sent to your HUD and then scan a series of waypoints along a designated route. The Vanduul ships in this sector are highly skilled and aggressive, so InterSec highly recommends that you <EM4>bring several combat-ready ships along as backup</EM4>.

InterSec understands the immense danger you'll be under on this patrol, so we'll send a special bonus of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) MG Scrip</EM4> to the main contractor once the sector is clear.      

Respectfully,

Deacon Tobin
Operations Manager
InterSec Defense Solutions

This communication consists of a non-binding offer that upon acceptance may be immediately terminated by either party without any given notice. All operations are conducted at the contractor’s own personal risk and must adhere to system law where applicable.

title: ENTRY LVL. COURIER NEEDED IN STANTON
description:
AVAILABLE FTL CONTRACT
FOR IMMEDIATE ASSIGNMENT

**CONTRACT DETAILS** 
A reliable and hardworking entry-level courier is needed to ferry a delivery in Stanton.
You will be responsible for providing your own transportation.
Interested contractors need the <EM4>ability to lift up to .25 SCU boxes</EM4>.
Contractors are employed on an independent basis and therefore not covered by FTL's company insurance.

~mission(Contractor|SignOff)

title: Preliminary Bounty: ~mission(TargetName)
description:
--------------------------------------------------------
----------------- BOUNTY CONTRACT -----------------
--------------------------------------------------------

BOUNTY HUNTERS GUILD
PROCESSING & DISTRIBUTION

The Bounty Hunters Guild has authorized an official bounty.

NAME: ~mission(TargetName) 
SYSTEM: Stanton
RISK ASSESSMENT: Medium
LAST KNOWN LOCATION:  ~mission(Location|Address)
KNOWN AFFILIATIONS: <None>
NOTES: This preliminary bounty is being offered to you as a formal evaluation of your skills. Successful completion of this contract will lead to additional bounty work being made available. 

It is believed that the target will be on foot and well armed, so be prepared to engage in a firefight. Additionally, there may be other hostiles forces at the site, while they should not be your primary focus, you may have to engage with them while in pursuit of the target.


BOUNTY HUNTERS GUILD
In Pursuit of Justice

The Bounty Hunters Guild is a security service sanctioned by the Mercenary Guild and authorized to operate in both the UEE and beyond in accordance with Xenotrade Authority Acts.

title: Verified Bounty: ~mission(TargetName) at QV Breaker Station
description:
--------------------------------------------------------
----------------- BOUNTY CONTRACT -----------------
--------------------------------------------------------

BOUNTY HUNTERS GUILD
PROCESSING & DISTRIBUTION

The Bounty Hunters Guild has authorized an official bounty.

NAME: ~mission(TargetName) 
SYSTEM: Nyx
RISK ASSESSMENT: Medium
LAST KNOWN LOCATION:  ~mission(Location|Address)
KNOWN AFFILIATIONS: Claw Salamanders
NOTES: These old, abandoned QV Breaker Stations should be approached with caution. Not only are they are in a state of significant disrepair, but they are frequently inhabited by dangerous fauna and outlaws, like the target's associate gang members.

NOTES: Because these abandoned QV Breaker Stations are not fully functional, there is a good chance that you will have to perform some repairs before your able to access the target's location. You may also have to deal with other hazards like dangerous fauna.

Additionally, the intel gathered on ~mission(TargetName) has them as a high ranking member of the Claw Salamanders, one who will not appear unless suitably drawn out of hiding. Engage with enough of their lower ranked associates and they will be forced to come deal with you themselves.

title: Sticky Fingers
description:
Headhunters are always on the lookout for talented folks that can quickly and discreetly acquire items that we have our eye on. If that’s you, I’ve got a job that should test your skills. Ace it and I have plenty more I can start sending your way.  

There’s a crate of ~mission(Item) currently at ~mission(Location|Address). Go liberate it from its current owners and deliver it to ~mission(Destination|Address). Should be quick and easy. 

- Stows

title: Grab Requested Goods
description:
One of our Judges put out a bulletin asking Headhunters to keep an eye on for ~mission(Item). Got word yesterday that some can be found at ~mission(Location|Address). Feel like making a high ranking Headhunter happy?

All you need to do is get your hands on the goods then run ‘em across system to ~mission(Destination|Address).

Stows out.

title: Commandeer Supplies
description:
Get this. There’s a new crew trying to make a name for themselves. From what I’ve heard, they’re in way over their head, but that didn’t stop them from spending some serious creds to acquire ~mission(Item).

Currently, some of them are holed up at ~mission(Location|Address). Go there, relieve them, of the ~mission(Item), and then bring it to ~mission(Destination|Address). Hopefully when word of what happened there spreads to the rest of the crew, they’ll reconsider their choices in life. 

Stows out.

title: Easy Pickings
description: Been looking for ~mission(Item), and one of our people inside CFP let me know some was just delivered to ~mission(Location|Address). Should only be a few soft CFP guards around for you to deal with. Then grab the shit and bring it to ~mission(Destination|Address).\n\nStows out.

title: Time to Resupply
description: Our crew at ~mission(Destination|Address) is running low on ~mission(Item). Want to get them restocked, and know that the CFP outpost at ~mission(Location|Address) has some lying around. Wanna go there and grab it for us?\n\n- Stows

title: Smash and Dash
description: Smash and Dash

title: Steal and Slip Away
description:
We’ve been watching ~mission(Location|Address) for a while. Felt like a primo XenoThreat spot to hit, since there are fewer guards around protecting the goods, but didn’t want to kick down the door until we knew something worthwhile was inside. Well, that glorious day has come.   

Got word that the latest delivery left ~mission(Item) there. We want to get our hands on it, so are looking for someone who can swipe it and then bring it to ~mission(Destination|Address). Sure the forces there won’t make it easy for you, but do this right and try to get out before anyone from the main outpost knows what’s going on.    

- Stows

title: Steal Some ~mission(Item)
description:
Less you know about this the better. Trust me. 

There’s some ~mission(Item) sitting at ~mission(Location|Address). Go deal with anyone there and steal it. Then hustle it over to ~mission(Destination|Address). 

Should be a breezy job and easy creds, so get it done. 

- Stows

description:

All you need to do is get your hands on the goods then run ‘em across system to ~mission(Destination|Address).

title: ~mission(Destination) Errand
description:
Got a box sitting at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> that needs to make its way over to <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>. Any chance you can knock this out real quick?

Might be the easiest gig I’ve ever assigned out. Only real threat would be tripping over your own feet. A <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> would also be smart to bring along. 

Stows out.

title: Little Heavy Lifting at ~mission(Destination)
description:
Got a bunch of boxes sitting at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> that have to be moved to <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>. Seems easy enough, right? Well, guess it ain’t. 

The idiot who was supposed to move these things threw out his back trying to carry all of ‘em at once. So, you know, maybe bring a <EM4> handheld tractor beam</EM4> and try taking ‘em one at a time like a normal person. But, hey, don’t let me tell you how to do your job. Just get it done. 

Stows out.

Got a crate that needs to get to <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>. There’s a Clip waiting there for it. They’re a real nasty bastard too, so best not mess this up. 

Just grab the package from <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. Don’t do anything dumb with it, and you’ll be fine. A <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> would also be smart to bring along.

title: Wrong Address
description:
Got a special delivery for a Headhunter. Problem is they were reassigned to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>, so I need someone to go to the main outpost, grab package <EM4>#~mission(Item1|SerialNumber)</EM4>, and take it to their new spot.   

Good news is the two aren’t too far apart from each other. You could hoof it between the two, if you really want to get your cardio in for the day. Otherwise, it should be quick and easy to fly or drive it over. 

A <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> would also be smart to bring along. 

Stows out.

title: Spread Some Supplies
description:
Got all our prospects at ~mission(Location) busy preppin’ for an upcoming job so there’s a bunch of boxes sitting there that need to be moved to other parts of the outpost. Feel like making some easy creds doing it for us? 

Everything can be picked up from <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. Then moved around the outpost to these spots:

description:
Seems like they can’t tell their ass from their elbow over at ~mission(Location) and the place is a mess. I need help collecting supplies spread across the outpost and putting them where they’re supposed to go. Here are the specifics:

Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item1|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item4|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item5|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location1|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item2|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location1|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item3|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location2|Address)</EM4>.
  
All of the above need to end up at <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>. Easy and low-risk work, if you’re interested.

A <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> would also be smart to bring along. 
 
Stows out.

title: Loaded with Loot
description:
Headhunters crews have been having a streak of good luck. I figure why take them off the job and stop them earning when I could just hire someone to deliver their spoils instead. Here’s the list of pick-ups needed:    

Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item1|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item4|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item5|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location1|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item2|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location1|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item3|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location2|Address)</EM4>.

Once you grab ‘em, everything needs to go to <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>.

Make sure it gets done and done right. A <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> would also be smart to bring along. 

Stows out.

Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item4|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item5|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location1|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item2|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location1|Address)</EM4>.
Pick up package <EM4>#~mission(Item3|SerialNumber)</EM4> at <EM4>~mission(Location2|Address)</EM4>.

Once you grab ‘em, everything needs to go to <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>.

Make sure it gets done and done right. A <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> would also be smart to bring along. 

Stows out.

title: Cave Raid
description:
CFP recently got a delivery of ~mission(Item) that we want. Think they know that too, because they hid it in a cave near ~mission(Location|Address) and have a handful of mercs watching over it. Let’s show ‘em that nothing gets by the Headhunters around here.  

Raid the cave, grabs the goods, and bring them to ~mission(Destination|Address). Sound like a plan?

- Stows

title: Cave Raid
description:
Some crew whose name doesn’t even deserve to cross my lips stole a bunch of our supplies, and then hid them in ~mission(Location|Address). As if we wouldn’t be able to track them down. Now that we know where they hid the goods it’s time to make them pay. 

Interested in raiding that cave and getting our shit back? Do your worst to whoever’s inside just don’t damage our goods. Then bring everything to  ~mission(Destination|Address). Sounds kinda fun. Makes me miss my days when I applied my other skills to help the gang.

Stows out.

title: Wrecked Ships for Sale
description:
Had a few wrecked ships towed to ~mission(Location|Address). They’re fully out of commission and in dire need of scrapping. If you’re interested in salvaging what’s left, it can be all yours, for a fair price, too.

Plus, you don’t have to worry much about any distractions while you’re workin’ since you’ll be near one of our stations and we make sure folks behave themselves.

- Smokey

title: Salvage Wrecked Ships on the Drift
description:
Got word there’s a few ships near ~mission(Location|Address) just floating all by their lonesome. It’s good salvage, but none of our crews have the time right now. We figured to give you first snap at it.

If you want the coords, we’ll sell them to you for a price. Then you’re free to pick the wreck as clean as you like and sell it all for a tidy profit. Now, you might run into some trouble out there from rival scavs, but I’m sure it won’t be anything you can’t handle.

- Smokey

title: Hauling ask.
description:
You been doing good work for us, so I got a favor to ask. I'll still pay you, but it's a favor. Got some cargo that needs to get bounced from <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> to <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4> and the guy that was supposed to do it found himself on the wrong side of an airlock, but hey, shit still needs to get done.

Only thing is you'll need a ship that can carry <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize) cargo containers</EM4>.  A <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> would also be smart to bring along. 

If you could sort it out for me, that'd be great.

- Stows out

title: Getting Our Goods Back
description:
We’ve been using the old Hathor <EM4>~mission(Location1|Address)</EM4> and <EM4>~mission(Location2|Address)</EM4> as stashes, but it turns out some over-eager nullbrains sniffed them out and are trying to boost our shit for their own.

Luckily, the goods are still there - for now. We need someone to visit that PAF and OLP, grab our stuff from the freight elevators, and take it to the freight elevator at <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4> where we should be able to keep it on ice. 

Here’s the list of what to grab. Don’t care what order you do ‘em in. Be sure to bring a ship that can store <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize)</EM4> at the very least. 

~mission(MultiToSingleToken)

Make sure you’re ready to deal with whatever assholes you run across. A <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> would also be smart to bring along. 

Stows out.

title: Stock Taking
description:
I won’t bore you with the details, all you need to know is: one of our haulers got attacked and now our goods are sitting in a freight elevator at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. 

Up to you how you get the goods out, but if you happen to ghost a few of our rivals in the process, I ain’t complaining. Once you’ve got the stuff take it to the freight elevator at <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>, then we can sort your payment. 

A <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> would also be smart to bring along. 

Stows out.

title: Cargo Retrieval
description:
Need someone to snag some cargo for us. We were using an old Hathor PAF for dead drops, but a local gang has dug in and we can’t get our shit.

If you feel like kicking some ass, grab the cargo from the freight elevator at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> and deliver it to the freight elevator at <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>. 

From what I’ve heard this place is full of all sorts. Won’t be easy getting out stuff back, but that’s what we’re paying you for.

Just make sure you got a ship that can handle a <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize)</EM4> load, and try not to get shot in the process. A <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> would also be smart to bring along. 

Stows out.

title: Scrap Salvage at ~mission(Location)
description:
Need a hand taking care of some scrap at ~mission(Location|Address). The team there put it all in one place but can’t be bothered to salvage it. Just clear it out for them to get some RMC and make some easy creds. 

- Stows

title: Shutdown CFP’s Comms
description:
CFP think they’re safe over at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> but we got big plans for them. You’re gonna pave the way for us by heading there and shutting down their comm towers.

There should be a <EM4>control terminal at the top</EM4> that you can use to shut the tower down. You get that sorted and they won’t be able to call for backup. Then we can show those skags who this system belongs to.

Most outposts use the same system, so this’ll come in handy next time you want to make sure reinforcements don’t mess with you.


Stows out.

title: Silence the Xenothreat
description:
We’re making a play to hit some XenoThreat assholes where it hurts but need to be smart about it. That’s why we’re sending you in first to take out their comm towers so they can’t call for backup. You do that and we can be in and out before they know it.

Head to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> and climb the comm towers there. Should be a <EM4>control panel at the top</EM4> you can use to switch them off.

In fact, same goes for most other outposts. Rather than having to deal with reinforcements, you can just turn off the comm towers. But hey, if you wanna ring the bell to ghost a few more Xenothreat I ain’t complaining.


Stows out.

title: Ground The Upstarts
description:
Headhunters didn't get to be what it is today by playing nice. We've tracked down the hideout of a bunch of scraggy upstarts that have been making inroads into our territory. Need you to send a message.

Head to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> and blow out of their fuel tanks. They'll think twice about muscling in on our operations when their ships are grounded.

-Stows out

title: No Fly Zone
description:
Some competition has taken root at ~mission(Location|Address) and they've really been gumming up the works for our operations in the area.

Now part of me sees some potential in the upstarts so I'm hoping to bring them into fold, but at the same time, I can't really afford to have them flying around causing trouble.

Instead, I want you to go and blow up their fuel tanks. That should keep them grounded long enough for me to talk some sense into their thick heads.

-Stows out

title: Stopping the Competition
description: Stopping the Competition

title: Wasting Their Stockpile
description:
Look, Headhunters don't waste time on petty revenge, but I'm not about to let this nonsense fly either.

Some asshole took out a bunch of my crew and has been bragging all over about it. I've also heard that this nullbrain is stockpiling quantum fuel over at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. I'd like to see how they deal with some real heat when you fly over there and burn everything they've built to the ground. Oh, and with the amount of firepower they got gathered over there, wouldn't be smart to fly alone on this one, so take a crew with you.

-Stows out

title: Trapping a Rat
description:
Got a plan to take care of a rat I've been dealing with and need a hand.

Had a client default on some debts and now they've decided they ain't going to pay up. Got a tip that they're hiding out over at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. If you can take out their fuel tanks they'll have no choice but to reconsider their crap decisions.

Just be sure you don't underestimate this slimebag. They might be dumb but they're not stupid, if you get me. I've heard this place is swarming with ships and turrets, you'd have to be a total nullbrain not to bring backup.

-Stows out

title: Outsmarting our Enemies
description:
We've heard news that a new gang are planning a bombing run on a place over at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. They figure if they can take out the fuel supplies there then it's theirs for the taking.

But little do they know that we're gonna send you in first to blow the fuel tanks so we can claim the place before they do. If it all goes to plan, we can clear the location out and stick it to that gang at the same time. Two birds, one stone.

Only problem is this place is armed to the teeth. Bring a crew and get those fuel tanks out of action. We'll take care of the rest.

-Stows out

title: Take Out Citizens for Prosperity Fuel Supply
description:
It seems like I can't go a day without waking up to one of my people bitchin' and moanin' about how hard Citizens for Prosperity is making their life. 

Eventually we're going wipe those do-gooders out of the system, but for now I'll settle for some peace and quiet. 

Go to ~mission(Location|Address) and destroy the fuel stores CFP have got there. That should at least keep them out of our business for a bit.

-Stows out

title: Wipe Citizens for Prosperity Data Farm
description:
Turns out those Citizens for Prosperity bastards have been gathering intel on us. They're going to try to cut us off from several revenue streams and bleed us dry. Can't let that happen. 

Head to ~mission(Location|Address) and destroy their data storage. 

That should teach them not to stick their noses where they don't belong.

-Stows out

title: Blow Up Citizens for Prosperity Fuel Reserves
description:
I'm getting pretty bloody sick of Citizens for Prosperity sticking their noses where they don't belong. 

Started thinking that it might be pretty nice to ground their fleet for a little bit. We blow up their fuel tanks at ~mission(Location|Address) and those little pissheads won't be going nowhere quick.

Sound like your kinda thing?

-Stows out

title: Stop Rivals from Launching Heist
description:
When will people learn that they can't just do whatever they feel like on Headhunter turf? These idiots are planning a big job and thought that I'd just let them get away with it? No way in hell.

I want you to fly to ~mission(Location|Address) and destroy their fuel tanks and data servers. Show them what the Headhunters really think of them.

Let's see them try to pull off anything after that.

-Stows out

title: Cut Citizens For Prosperity Power
description:
We got big plans about to go down but unfortunately Citizens for Prosperity have people nearby at  ~mission(Location|Address).

Since I can't afford to have those bastards cockin' things up for us, I want you to go there and take out their power generators.

Should keep them sidelined until we're done with our business. 

-Stows out

title: Wipe Citizens for Prosperity Data Farm
description:

title: Wipe Out XenoThreat Servers
description:
Something just fell into my lap that is way too good to pass up. XenoThreat's intelligence gathering is a bit of legend in the circles I run in and it turns out that they're storing a whole heap of their best intel at ~mission(Location|Address).

Would be a shame if someone, say like you, were to go there and blow it all up, huh? 

-Stows out

-Stows out

title: Blow Up XenoThreat Fuel Reserves
description:
It seems that XenoThreat has gotten the same intel we did and are looking to pull off the same job as us. 

I want you to knock the competition out of the picture. Go to ~mission(Location|Address) and take out the fuel tanks they got there.

Then my people can finish up without worrying about XenoThreat crashing the party.

-Stows out

title: Wipe Citizens for Prosperity Data Farm
description:
Turns out those Citizens for Prosperity bastards have been gathering intel on us. They're going to try to cut us off from several revenue streams and bleed us dry. Can't let that happen. 

Head to ~mission(Location|Address) and destroy their data storage. 

That should teach them not to stick their noses where they don't belong.

-Stows out

title: Clear Citizens for Prosperity Servers
description:
Long story, short - a Headhunter messed up real bad and now Citizens for Prosperity has gotten their hands on some data that could really do some damage to us. 

The bastard is going to need some time to heal so that leaves it to you to clean up the mess. Can you go to ~mission(Location|Address) and destroy the servers they got stored there? I wanna make sure there's no chance of this info getting out there.

-Stows out

title: Purge XenoThreat Servers
description:
I'm so mad right now I can barely see straight. A Headhunter who I thought I could trust just went over to the XenoThreat. Yeah, aliens killed your family. Shit sucks, but you don't turn your back on your people. 

To add insult to injury, the bastard gave XenoThreat a bunch of our intel. I have some other folks tracking the traitor down, but I need you to take care of wiping out the stolen data for me. It's being kept on servers at ~mission(Location|Address).

-Stows out

title: Wipe Out XenoThreat Servers
description:
Something just fell into my lap that is way too good to pass up. XenoThreat's intelligence gathering is a bit of legend in the circles I run in and it turns out that they're storing a whole heap of their best intel at ~mission(Location|Address).

Would be a shame if someone, say like you, were to go there and blow it all up, huh? 

-Stows out

title: Destroy XenoThreat Fuel Reserves
description:
I won't get into the long and complicated politics of why I'm asking you to do this, but it's enough for you to know that I want it done. 

Go to ~mission(Location|Address) and blow up the XenoThreat fuel reserves there. It'll really be helping me out of a tight spot.

-Stows

title: Blow Up XenoThreat Fuel Reserves
description:
It seems that XenoThreat has gotten the same intel we did and are looking to pull off the same job as us. 

I want you to knock the competition out of the picture. Go to ~mission(Location|Address) and take out the fuel tanks they got there.

Then my people can finish up without worrying about XenoThreat crashing the party.

-Stows out

title: Blow Up Citizens for Prosperity Fuel Reserves
description:
I'm getting pretty bloody sick of Citizens for Prosperity sticking their noses where they don't belong. 

Started thinking that it might be pretty nice to ground their fleet for a little bit. We blow up their fuel tanks at ~mission(Location|Address) and those little pissheads won't be going nowhere quick.

Sound like your kinda thing?

-Stows out

title: Take Out Citizens for Prosperity Fuel Supply
description:
It seems like I can't go a day without waking up to one of my people bitchin' and moanin' about how hard Citizens for Prosperity is making their life. 

Eventually we're going wipe those do-gooders out of the system, but for now I'll settle for some peace and quiet. 

Go to ~mission(Location|Address) and destroy the fuel stores CFP have got there. That should at least keep them out of our business for a bit.

-Stows out

title: No Fly Zone
description:
Some competition has taken root at ~mission(Location|Address) and they've really been gumming up the works for our operations in the area.

Now part of me sees some potential in the upstarts so I'm hoping to bring them into fold, but at the same time, I can't really afford to have them flying around causing trouble.

Instead, I want you to go and blow up their fuel tanks. That should keep them grounded long enough for me to talk some sense into their thick heads.

-Stows out

title: Shut Off Power at ~mission(Location)
description: This is a fairly straightforward job. We got some assholes at ~mission(Location|Address) who have been thinking they're better than they are. Go and shut off their power to remind them that Headhunters are in charge.\n\n-Stows out

title: Knock Power Out
description:
Got a few scavs that have rooted themselves into ~mission(Location|Address). We have our own plans there and need the place cleared out. 

That's why I want you to ho and turn off their power. Let's see if that doesn't cause these rats to abandon the place for good.

-Stows

title: Convince Upstarts to Abandon Sector
description:
Everyone has been talking about a new player named ~mission(TargetName) who is operating out of ~mission(Location|Address). They've become a real concern for the Headhunters. 

It's time to shut them down. 

Head over, take out ~mission(TargetName|Last) and then clear out any fuel stores they have there to convince anyone remaining to leave. 

Figure after you pull this off, nobody will even remember their name.

-Stows

title: Teach Citizens for Prosperity A Lesson
description:
Every day it seems like Citizens for Prosperity is out here trying to snatch up more people for their shitty little cause and make life more difficult for hardworkin' folks like ourselves. 

I figure they could use a reminder about who really is in charge here. Head to ~mission(Location|Address) and bomb the living hell out of the area. 

Nothing like a bit of property damage to get a point across.

-Stows out

title: New Opportunity - Ambush Strike
description:
ECKHART SECURITY, LLC.
CONTRACTS & DISPATCH

CONTRACT TYPE: Ship Ambush
APPROVAL CODE = ~mission(ApprovalCode)

FOR IMMEDIATE PROCESSING

We’re looking for contractors with combat experience who are willing to get their hands dirty so to speak. A client of ours was double-crossed by a <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4> and we’ve been waiting for an opportunity to deal with the issue. After tracking them for several weeks, we’ve identified <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> as the ideal spot to intercept them. It features an <EM4>emission cloud</EM4> where you can hide and power down your ship while waiting for the <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4>.

Intel suggests that they may travel with an escort. If they do, you’ll have to deal with them too. Since this is your first contract, we will have other contractors in the area who can lend a hand once you strike.

Payment will be issued upon successful completion of contract. There’s a bonus payment of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) MG Scrip</EM4> in this for the main contractor too.

This contract is non-negotiable.

This message may contain confidential, proprietary, privileged and/or private information. The information is intended to be for the use of the individual or entity designated above.

title: Settle a Score
description:
ECKHART SECURITY, LLC.
CONTRACTS & DISPATCH

CONTRACT TYPE: Ship Ambush
APPROVAL CODE = ~mission(ApprovalCode)

FOR IMMEDIATE PROCESSING

One of our clients has been the victim of consistent harassment so the time has come to send a clear cease-and-desist message by taking out their prized <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4>.  The lowlife in question follows a fairly predictable routine making them susceptible to an ambush. We’ve identified <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> as the ideal spot. It features an <EM4>emission cloud</EM4> where you can hide and power down your ship while waiting. 

By the way, the <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4> often flies alone but is sometimes joined by a friend in a single-seat ship. Neither should give you too much trouble, but we have other contractors in the area who can lend a hand once you strike.

Payment will be issued upon successful completion of contract. There’s a bonus payment of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) MG Scrip</EM4> in this for the main contractor too.

This contract is non-negotiable.

This message may contain confidential, proprietary, privileged and/or private information. The information is intended to be for the use of the individual or entity designated above.

title: Time to Teach a Lesson
description:
ECKHART SECURITY, LLC.
CONTRACTS & DISPATCH

CONTRACT TYPE: Ship Ambush
APPROVAL CODE = ~mission(ApprovalCode)

FOR IMMEDIATE PROCESSING

A client of ours has been receiving anonymous threats in an attempt to blackmail them, and after a bit of leg work on our part, we’ve managed to trace it back to an individual who pilots a particular <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4>. The client wants us to take care of this blackmailer before they have a chance to make good. 

Based on our assessment, the target shouldn’t be too difficult to handle. They fly by <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> on the regular, which has an <EM4>emission cloud</EM4> that’s an ideal spot to power down your ship and lie in wait. Sometimes the target is alone but they often travel with other single-seat ships as protection. Best be strategic when you launch your ambush or the <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4> could have a chance to get away. We don’t want to lose this one. We’ll alert other contractors in the area of the ambush so they can come help once it pops off.     

Payment will be issued upon successful completion of contract. The client has also added a bonus payment of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) MG Scrip</EM4> for the main contractor.

This contract is non-negotiable.

This message may contain confidential, proprietary, privileged and/or private information. The information is intended to be for the use of the individual or entity designated above.

title: Launch Surprise Attack
description:
ECKHART SECURITY, LLC.
CONTRACTS & DISPATCH

CONTRACT TYPE: Ship Ambush
APPROVAL CODE = ~mission(ApprovalCode)

FOR IMMEDIATE PROCESSING

Need someone to take out a <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4> and deal with the escort ships protecting it. They’ve already interfered with several of our ongoing operations and have become a real problem across several of Eckhart Security cases. Not sure what their endgame is, but it won’t matter once you deal with them.

We’ve let slip that we will be working a job over at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> which should serve as bait to draw them out. The spot is ideal for an ambush because of the <EM4>emission cloud</EM4> that’s a perfect place to power down and hide until you’re ready to strike. If you’re available, the <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4> and its escorts should be passing through soon.   

Payment will be issued upon successful completion of contract. The main contractor will also receive a bonus payment of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) MG Scrip</EM4>.

This contract is non-negotiable.

This message may contain confidential, proprietary, privileged and/or private information. The information is intended to be for the use of the individual or entity designated above.

title: Tricky Ambush
description:
ECKHART SECURITY, LLC.
CONTRACTS & DISPATCH

CONTRACT TYPE: Ship Ambush
APPROVAL CODE = ~mission(ApprovalCode)

FOR IMMEDIATE PROCESSING

Looking for a competent contractor who can recruit a wingman to help execute an ambush. The target is a <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4> which has been involved in several operations against one of our most important clients. By neutralizing it once and for all, it’ll make our client’s life a hell of a lot easier. While the ~mission(AmbushTarget) is our focus, they usually don’t fly anywhere without a sizable escort, including large craft. 

We’ve gotten word that this group will be passing by <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> soon. Any chance you could hurry and beat them there? That spot has an <EM4>emission cloud</EM4> that you can power down your ship and hide in. Then when they pass, you should have the upper hand.  

Payment will be issued upon successful completion of contract. The client knows this one will be tough, so they threw in a bonus payment of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) MG Scrip</EM4> that’ll go to the main contractor.

This contract is non-negotiable.

This message may contain confidential, proprietary, privileged and/or private information. The information is intended to be for the use of the individual or entity designated above.

title: Dangerous Ambush
description:
ECKHART SECURITY, LLC.
CONTRACTS & DISPATCH

CONTRACT TYPE: Ship Ambush
APPROVAL CODE = ~mission(ApprovalCode)

FOR IMMEDIATE PROCESSING

A high profile client recently lost an important shipment when a pilot double-crossed them and ran off with their product. After weeks of hunting we’ve discovered that they were connected with an outlaw pack, so the client is looking to us to deter similar future issues from arising. The ultimate target is the pilot of a <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4>, who leads the crew responsible. This won’t be easy. They don’t go anywhere without escort fighters and sub-capital ships. That’s why we highly recommend that you find a trusted friend or two who can handle this with you.  
  
Intel gathered by our analysts indicates that they frequently fly by an <EM4>emission cloud</EM4> near <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> which would provide an excellent area to stage a surprise strike.

Payment will be issued upon successful completion of contract. We’re also adding a bonus payment of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) MG Scrip</EM4> that’ll go to the main contractor.

This contract is non-negotiable.

This message may contain confidential, proprietary, privileged and/or private information. The information is intended to be for the use of the individual or entity designated above.

title: Highly Dangerous Ambush
description:
ECKHART SECURITY, LLC.
CONTRACTS & DISPATCH

CONTRACT TYPE: Ship Ambush
APPROVAL CODE = ~mission(ApprovalCode)

FOR IMMEDIATE PROCESSING

Recently, we sent a contractor after a <EM4>~mission(AmbushTarget)</EM4>, who was leading a crew targeting the operations of an extremely important client. Unfortunately, it was a trap and we lost some good people. We can’t let the target get away again, so we’ve decided the best course of action is to give them a dose of their own medicine.  

Ambushing this target seems like the safest course of action, and we’ve identified a good spot for such an attack. There’s an <EM4>emission cloud</EM4> near <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> that the target and their crew frequently fly by. You can power down your ship and hide there until you’re ready to strike. This crew is ruthless and extremely skilled. Don’t make the same mistake as the previous contractor and go this alone. It would be best for you to bring a few trusted friends to help handle this crew.    

Payment will be issued upon successful completion of contract. Plus, we’ll throw in a bonus payment of <EM4>~mission(ScripAmount) MG Scrip</EM4> for the main contractor for taking care of this.

This contract is non-negotiable.

This message may contain confidential, proprietary, privileged and/or private information. The information is intended to be for the use of the individual or entity designated above.

title: ~mission(Location) needs massive repairs
description:
Down at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>, the place got utterly smashed in a storm and needs some serious patchwork.

If anyone out there has a <EM4>bunch of fuses</EM4> (I hear <EM4>six</EM4> should be plenty), a <EM4>salvaging tool</EM4>, and <EM4>enough RMC cans to deal with eight or so messed up pipes</EM4>, we'll hook you up with some creds to do the fix.

Hopefully you know what you're doing, but just in case, the <EM4>“Guide to Maintenance”</EM4> in your mobiGlas Journal might be worth a look.

Stows out.

title: Anybody Seen ~mission(TargetName)?
description:
So, I sent one of our people, a new recruit by the name ~mission(TargetName), to do a hand-off over at ~mission(Location|Address), but now the bastard’s gone missing.

I’m thinking they either ran off with Headhunter credits and they’re good as dead, or they were robbed and now they’re lying there actually good as dead. Either way, I’d like for you to go have a look and see if you can find them.

If you settle the matter, I’ll give you what their cut would have been.

-Stows

title: Track Down ~mission(TargetName)
description:
Some of my people were involved with a raid over at ~mission(Location|Address). Most of them got out clean, but after the dust settled nobody is sure of what happened to ~mission(TargetName).

A lot of other gangs would just leave the poor bastard to rot, but that’s not the way the Headhunters do things. I want to know for sure if ~mission(TargetName|Last) is alive or dead and I’m willing to pay you good credits to find out.

Just be careful when you get there because I bet the assholes who live there are still gunning for a fight.

- Stows

title: Track Down ~mission(TargetName)
description:
A few days back I dispatched ~mission(TargetName|Last) to scope out a cave system for potential use as a storehouse. Haven't heard from them since. 

My gut tells me they ran into trouble. The local wildlife isn't known for being friendly, and ~mission(TargetName|Last) isn't exactly known for being cautious. If you're in need of some creds, I'm offering some up for anyone who can get in there and figure out what happened. 

Stows out.

description:

Whether it's good news or bad, there are some creds in it for you. 

Stows out.

title: Find ~mission(TargetName|Last)\n
description:
Last night, some no-name drifters threw down with our enforcers and ran when things got too hot. Didn't find out 'til they were long gone, but they made off with one of our Prospects when they split. Goes by the name of ~mission(TargetName). 

Last we saw, they were headed in the direction ~mission(Location|Address). We need someone to head there and track down our missing Prospect. 

If you take any of them down while you're at it, all the better.

Stows out.

title: Track Down ~mission(TargetName)
description:
Those XenoThreat assholes ambushed one of our outposts. We eventually kicked their ass out of there but once the cowards knew they'd been beat, they took ~mission(TargetName) hostage as a Human shield. We let 'em go, because we don't betray our own like that, but we're not planning on letting this stand. 

Word is that they fled to their base at ~mission(Location|Address). We need someone to break in, find ~mission(TargetName|Last), and relay the info back to me. There's a lot of creds in it for you if you do the job right. 

Keep in mind this isn't their only outpost in the system. If they give you the slip, you may need to track them to another sector. 

Stows out.

title: Find Headhunter Crewmember and Help Finish the Job
description:
You ever get a bad feeling that you just can't shake? I had sent ~mission(TargetName) to take out the some XenoThreat servers at ~mission(Location|Address) and I'm starting think they ran into some serious trouble.

Do me a favor, huh? Go on over and see if you can figure out what happened to them and make sure that those servers are destroyed for good.

-Stows out

title: Find Headhunter Crewmember and Help Finish the Job
description:
I'm a bit at the end of my rope. I had sent ~mission(TargetName), a pretty dependable Headhunter, out to destroy the XenoThreat data servers at ~mission(Location|Address).

That was a while ago and I still haven't heard from them and the servers are still sitting there intact. 

Do me a favor, huh? Go on over and see if you can figure out what happened to ~mission(TargetName|NickOrFirst) and help finish destroying the servers.

-Stows out

title: Find Headhunter Crewmember and Help Finish the Job
description:
I'm not having the best day so far. I sent ~mission(TargetName) out to do a bit of destruction at ~mission(Location|Address)  and I haven't heard from them. 

Do me a favor, huh? Go on over and see if you can figure out what happened to ~mission(TargetName|NickOrFirst). And whatever the case, I want you to make sure their job is finished.

-Stows out

title: Unfinished Contract
description:
Here's the deal, one of our usual heavy hitters, a cat named ~mission(TargetName), was sent to ~mission(Location|Address) to knock out some XenoThreat who'd been hiding there. We knew it was going to be a tall order, but that's why we called in ~mission(TargetName|Last). Problem is, we ain't heard from them.

As far as we know XenoThreat are still operating out of that outpost so we gotta assume that ~mission(TargetName|Last) got popped. We need you to go in and finish what they started. Once that's done, if you can find out what happened to ~mission(TargetName|Last), that would be a bonus.

Stows out.

title: Should'a left it to a professional
description:
We had this kid named ~mission(TargetName) volunteer to go take out some XenoThreat near ~mission(Location|Address). Everything we knew about the place said it was gonna be too much for ~mission(TargetName|Last) to handle, but they were real keen on boosting their standing with the Headhunters and thought this would do it, so they went alone.

Haven't heard anything but XenoThreat comms coming out of that place since. If you want to go and knock it over, we'll pay you instead. If you can find out what happened to ~mission(TargetName|Last) that would be a bonus.

Stows out.

title: Bring the hammer down
description:
We tasked one of our up-and-coming triggers named ~mission(TargetName) to go clean out a hideout over at ~mission(Location|Address) from some assholes who'd been attacking some of the locals in the area. Well, I guess they got the drop on ~mission(TargetName|Last) because we haven't heard shit since.

I want you to go and finish the job. While you're out there, if you can find out what happened to ~mission(TargetName|Last), that'd be great. I'm sure their dad would like to know.

Stows out.
