-Twitch

title: DEAD SAINTS - ~mission(ReputationRank) Rank - ~mission(CargoGradeToken) Cargo Haul
description:
We've got a shipment of contraband that needs to be hauled to a couple of different places. If you like creds, head to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>, load everything up, and bring it to these drop points:  

DROP OFF LOCATIONS (ANY ORDER)

~mission(SingleToMultiToken)

You're gonna need a <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> to move the boxes and enough room in your cargo hold for size <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize)</EM4> containers. 

Avoid other ships if you can. Security will bust you if they figure out what you're hauling, and raiders will just take it for themselves. 

-Twitch

title: DEAD SAINTS - ~mission(ReputationRank) Rank - ~mission(CargoGradeToken) Cargo Haul
description:
One of our best distributors got busted in Stanton so we need a replacement. The guy was an incredible salesman, but a shit smuggler. We need someone who won't run face-first into a security patrol or a pack of pirates to pick up some merchandise at these locations:

PICK UP LOCATIONS (ANY ORDER) 

~mission(MultiToSingleToken)

And bring it all to <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4> before security catches on to what's happening. You'll need enough space on your ship for size <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize)</EM4> containers, and a <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> should make loading and unloading nice and easy. 

-Twitch

title: DEAD SAINTS - ~mission(ReputationRank) Rank - ~mission(CargoGradeToken) Cargo Haul
description:
We're looking for someone discreet to move a new batch of altruciatoxin to a number of buyers . Yeah, it's illegal, but only if you get caught. 

The shipment is at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. You'll need to take it to these drop points:

DROP OFF LOCATIONS (ANY ORDER)

~mission(SingleToMultiToken)

Be sure your ship can carry size <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize)</EM4> containers, and don't forget to pack a <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4>. Not sure how you're planning on moving the boxes, otherwise.

-Twitch

title: DEAD SAINTS -- ~mission(ReputationRank) Rank, ~mission(CargoGradeToken) Scale Cargo Run
description:
If you're looking for creds, the Saints need someone with hauling experience to do a supply run from <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> to <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>. 

You're gonna need a ship that can hold size <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize) cargo containers</EM4> and a <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> to move the boxes around. 

Don't open the crates but just so you know, most of this stuff is legal but not all of it. Try not to cross paths with anyone who might make that a problem for you. Get me?

-Twitch

title: DEAD SAINTS - ~mission(ReputationRank) Rank - ~mission(CargoGradeToken) Cargo Haul
description:
If you've got a fast ship, can discreetly carry size <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize)</EM4> containers, and love getting a huge payout, we need you to head to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> immediately and take what you find there to these drop points:

DROP OFF LOCATIONS (ANY ORDER) 

~mission(SingleToMultiToken)  

The buyers are paying a premium to get their hands on fresh stormwal meat. Stay sharp. If security catches you with this, it's your ass on the line. If you're smart, you'll use a <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> to get everything loaded up quick. 

-Twitch

title: DEAD SAINTS -- ~mission(ReputationRank) Rank, ~mission(CargoGradeToken) Scale Cargo Run
description:
Got an interesting one. Some folks who were suckered into life/labor contracts with Hurston Dynamics want to get as far from Lorville as they possibly can. They struck a deal with a people mover and got themselves put on ice. Now they just need someone to pick them up and give them a ride. 

If you're up for some popsicle smuggling, you'll find ~mission(total) cryopods at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> that need to go to <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>. Make sure you have space in your cargo hold for size <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize)</EM4> containers. If you use a <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4>, you should be able to get everyone loaded up before Hurston security catches on. 

HD isn't too keen on anyone slipping out of their contracts, so watch your back. They shoot to kill. 

If you can pull this off, there's a nice paycheck in it for you.

-Twitch

title: First Haul in System
description:
Welcome to the system, not sure if you're familiar with our organization but Citizens for Prosperity are a non-profit organization dedicated to bringing security and civilization to unclaimed systems.

If you're looking for work in the system and would like to help achieve our goal, we need a package picked up from <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> and taken to <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>.

Once complete, we can offer you a variety of new job opportunities in this system and beyond.

Lima Endicott
Lead Dispatcher
Citizens for Prosperity

title: Need a Hauler
description:
Hi, we need to move some cargo across the system and were wondering if you could help. I know this is outside your usual purview, but I'm low on options. This would also be a great way to show your committed to Citizens for Prosperity's greater mission here.

If you head to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>, there should be some cargo accessible via a freight elevator that we'll need you to take to another freight elevator at <EM4>~mission(Destination|Address)</EM4>.

Only thing to call out is that your ship must be able to handle <EM4>~mission(MissionMaxSCUSize) cargo containers</EM4>. To make your life easier, be sure you have a <EM4>handheld tractor beam</EM4> with you. Otherwise, it should be a simple run. 

Lima Endicott
Lead Dispatcher
Citizens for Prosperity

title: Clear ~mission(Location) of Salvage
description:
Hi,

A repair crew has been making some much needed changes and upgrades to our outposts. After finishing some work at ~mission(Location|Address), they've set aside some scrap they didn’t have time to deal with. 

Looking to hire someone to go there and salvage it so it’s out of the way. Should be a quick clean up if you’re interested. Any RMC is yours to keep.

Thanks,

Lima Endicott
Lead Dispatcher
Citizens for Prosperity

Looking to hire someone to go there and salvage it so it’s out of the way. Should be a quick clean up if you’re interested. Any RMC is yours to keep.

Thanks,

Lima Endicott
Lead Dispatcher
Citizens for Prosperity

title: Disable Headhunter Comm Towers
description:
Violence is never our first choice, but sometimes it must be used as a preventative measure.

We’re planning a strike on a Headhunter controlled outpost and need your help.

We need to make sure that if something goes wrong they won’t be able to call for backup and escalate the situation. That’s where you come in. We need you to head over to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> and disable the communication towers. There should be a <EM4>control panel located at the top</EM4> of each tower that you can use to deactivate them.

If you’re able to do this, Citizens for Prosperity will be able to gain some vital ground in Pyro.

It’s also worth keeping in mind for future contracts that you can choose to take out the comms towers instead of risking a fight with reinforcement dropships.

Stay alert and stay safe.

Thank you,

Lima Endicott
Lead Dispatcher
Citizens for Prosperity

title: Shutdown Xenothreat Comm Towers
description:
Citizens for Prosperity are eager to help the people of Pyro, and it starts with freeing them from the grasp of XenoThreat.

We’re preparing a team to infiltrate one of their outposts, but we need someone to go in ahead to take out their communication towers first. Once those towers are offline, they won’t be able to call for reinforcements and we can proceed with our plan.

Head to <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4> and find the towers. At the <EM4>top is a control panel</EM4> that can be used to disable them.

Also, it’s worth noting that most outposts use a similar system, so you’ll know how to disable the comms towers rather than risk fighting reinforcement dropships when you’re doing other jobs.

Good luck,

Lima Endicott
Lead Dispatcher
Citizens for Prosperity

title: Bounty Assignment: ~mission(TargetName) (HRT)
description:
Hey, 

Have an interesting bounty for you. A high level criminal by the name ~mission(TargetName) has been located at ~mission(Location|Address). 

Based on our intel, its highly unlikely that the target’s going to be sitting and waiting out in the open. You most likely will have to infiltrate the facility and draw them out. 

Something like a large firefight should be enough to do the trick. 

Best of luck,

Braden Corchado
Logistics Officer - Stanton Branch
Northrock Service Group

title: Bounty Assignment: ~mission(TargetName) (HRT)
description:
Hey, 

Got a contract for you that requires a bit more tactics than usual. A high-level criminal by the name ~mission(TargetName) is hiding with a large contingent of outlaws at ~mission(Location|Address). 

Our intel suggests that ~mission(TargetName|Last)’s forces most likely will act as defensive line in order to protect their boss. My best guess is that you will have to deal with them before you gain access to ~mission(TargetName|Last).

That said, I don’t want you shooting indiscriminately. Sometimes these outlaws keep civilians around and they should be treated like non-combatants.

Best of luck,

Braden Corchado
Logistics Officer - Stanton Branch
Northrock Service Group

title: Pro Tem Bounty Assignment: ~mission(TargetName)
description:
Hey,

Northrock recently received intel that a bounty who disappeared from Stanton is back. <EM4>~mission(TargetName)</EM4> has been spotted at <EM4>~mission(Location|Address)</EM4>. Surveillance of the orbital platform indicates that <EM4>~mission(TargetName|Last)</EM4> is directing the operation there. Getting to ‘em won’t be easy, but absolutely essential.   

We’re determined to not let them get away again. That’s why we’re giving anyone interested the opportunity to apprehend this target. Just be prepared to face fierce resistance. 


Best of luck,

Braden Corchado
Logistics Officer - Stanton Branch
Northrock Service Group

title: URGENT: Boarding Action In Progress
description:
An executive client just informed us that their 890 Jump had been intercepted at ~mission(Location) and boarded by outlaws. They would prefer to have this handled privately owing to the sensitive nature of proprietary data that's being stored aboard. 

Your first priority will be to clear all outlaws as quickly as possible so that a recovery team can be dispatched to return the ship to the owner. The longer the outlaws have control of the vessel, the higher the chance it will be lost. I recommend you acquire a team for this outing to expedite matters. 

Note that thanks to a small contingent of Security Personnel who stayed aboard, all surviving members of the crew were able to flee in an escape craft. The current status of the remaining security is unknown, but just in case, keep an eye out for friendlies when you engage the hostiles.

Secondly, if at all possible, the client is *very* interested in preventing the theft of the aforementioned data. If you are able to stop the theft of their confidential data stores before they are transmitted, a generous bonus will be issued.

Needless to say, this is a client I would very much like to keep happy. 

Braden Corchado
Logistics Officer - Stanton Branch
Northrock Service Group
